user_message: What's at risk across my portfolio? Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 08:13 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 14:10 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Quantum Analytics
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Quantum Analytics)

[00:02:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:05:00] Priya (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Priya (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Priya (Quantum Analytics): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:23:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:28:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
